behaviors.description: All direct and transitive dependencies are extracted from the manifest and included in the vulnerability check.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this failure commonly appears at module handoff boundaries (manifest parser -> dependency graph/transitive resolver -> vulnerability-check input), not just inside one function. REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess is required to catch it; fixture manifests/lockfiles and in-process wiring are sufficient. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: no AI model decisions. REJECTION-GATE: System was considered but rejected because behavior text only requires extraction/inclusion logic, not real external infrastructure.
behaviors.test_description: Run scanner modules together on fixture manifests plus lockfiles containing both direct and transitive deps; assert the final vulnerability-check payload includes the complete dependency set with expected versions.

behaviors.description: The vulnerability database query returns results that match the exact version ranges in the manifest, not just the package name.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: this behavior crosses a real external API contract boundary (query encoding, range semantics, API-side matching). A stub can hide real failures where the live API interprets ranges differently. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: no AI model decisions and no multi-agent chain. REJECTION-GATE: Integration was considered but rejected because behavior text requires correctness of returned query results against exact ranges, which depends on real API behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Against a real vulnerability DB endpoint (or official staging), send queries for packages with multiple vulnerable/non-vulnerable versions and assert returned CVEs match manifest version ranges exactly, not name-only matches.

behaviors.description: The severity sorting places Critical above High above Medium above Low, with CVEs of the same severity sorted by CVSS score.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: behavior is pure ordering logic in one local comparator/sort function. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess needed. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: no AI dependency. REJECTION-GATE: Integration was considered but rejected because behavior text is strictly deterministic sorting rules.
behaviors.test_description: Feed unsorted CVE records with mixed severities and CVSS ties into the sorter and assert exact output order: severity rank first, then descending CVSS within severity.

behaviors.description: The manifest modification changes only the affected dependency's version, leaving all other entries unchanged.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: risk appears in parser/editor/serializer interaction (targeted edit vs unintended rewrites), not just a single function. REAL-INFRA: local fixture files and in-process modules are enough; no real network/subprocess required. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: System was considered but rejected because behavior text does not require OS-specific file events or external systems, only correctness of local manifest transformation.
behaviors.test_description: Run manifest-update pipeline on real fixture manifests (including formatting/ordering edge cases) and assert only the specified dependency version changes while all other entries and structure remain byte-equivalent where expected.

behaviors.description: The auto-generated pull request is created on the real repository with the correct single-dependency diff.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: behavior explicitly requires real repository + real PR creation, involving network/API auth and possibly git subprocess behavior. Mocks can pass while failing on live repo permissions, branch handling, or diff formation. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: no model-output dependency. REJECTION-GATE: Integration was rejected by concrete text: 'created on the real repository'.
behaviors.test_description: Execute scanner against a real test repository and verify a PR is actually opened; assert PR diff contains exactly one dependency-version change and no unrelated file modifications.

behaviors.description: A major version bump is correctly identified as a breaking change based on semver comparison.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is deterministic semver comparison logic at a local function boundary. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. REJECTION-GATE: higher levels are disqualified because behavior text is purely semantic version classification.
behaviors.test_description: Test semver comparator with representative pairs (e.g., 1.x->2.x breaking, 1.2->1.3 non-breaking, prerelease/build metadata cases) and assert breaking-change boolean.

behaviors.description: When a breaking change is detected, PR creation is skipped and a review flag is surfaced in the scanner output.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is orchestration/state-handoff across modules (semver decision -> PR workflow gate -> reporter output). REAL-INFRA: can be validated with in-process stubs for PR client/output sink; no real network is required by behavior text. INTEGRATION-BRAKE checks: (a) no required real OS/network/subprocess in text, (b) no AI/ML output dependence, (c) no multi-agentic chain, (d) no LLM-involved handoff. REJECTION-GATE: System rejected because behavior says 'skipped' and 'surfaced in scanner output' rather than requiring a real repository operation.
behaviors.test_description: Wire scanner decision engine, PR service adapter (stubbed), and report generator; trigger a major bump and assert PR adapter is not called while output includes the explicit review-required flag.
